title: feat: migrate plugin to TypeScript and ship a built artifact
description: Move all src/ JS to TypeScript, emit a typed CommonJS build to dist/, switch the npm publish surface to the build output, and integrate the build step into CI. Outcome: consumers get types out of the box (import FriendlyErrorsWebpackPlugin from '@soda/friendly-errors-webpack-plugin'), source contracts (transformer/formatter signatures, plugin options) are checked at compile time, and the release workflow has a real npm run build step. Tracked as four child beads so it can land in slices the same way the webpack-5 modernization did.

title: ci: set up GitHub Actions release workflow (npm publish, no GitHub Releases) — paired with user
description: Follow-up, done together with the repo owner because it needs the NPM_TOKEN secret and benefits from pair-debugging the first live release. Create .github/workflows/release.yml: push-on-master trigger, contents: write permission (for tag + changelog commit), checkout with fetch-depth: 0, setup-node, npm ci, run semantic-release via a GitHub Action that brings semantic-release + plugins as extra_plugins. Verify with a feat: commit (should publish to npm + tag + commit CHANGELOG.md) and a chore: commit (should NOT release). Confirm via 'npm view @soda/friendly-errors-webpack-plugin' and 'git ls-remote --tags'. Separate from Slice 3 because it requires user assistance.

title: Modernize plugin to webpack 5 and semantic-release
description: Bring @soda/friendly-errors-webpack-plugin up to date: drop webpack 4, remove internal-API imports (webpack/lib/RequestShortener, compiler.plugin() fallback), upgrade test/lint toolchain, bump Node floor to match webpack 5, and adopt semantic-release for CI-driven conventional-commits publishing to npm. Tracks the three-slice plan in docs/MODERNIZATION.md. Outcome: published 2.0.0 targeting webpack 5 only, auto-released from master.

title: chore(ts): point package.json publish surface at dist/
description: Switch the published artifact from raw src/ to the TypeScript build output. main: 'dist/index.js', types: 'dist/index.d.ts', files: ['dist'] (was ['src','index.js']). Add a 'prepack' script that runs npm run build so npm pack and @semantic-release/npm always publish a fresh build. Verify npm pack --dry-run shows only dist/ + package.json + README.md + LICENSE. Acceptance: local consumer smoke (npm pack tarball, install in /tmp dummy project, require() resolves and .d.ts is found by tsserver). No src/ or tsconfig.json in the published tarball.
acceptance_criteria: npm pack --dry-run shows only dist/ + manifest files; consumer smoke (require + import) works with type resolution

title: ci: run the TypeScript build in test and release workflows
description: Update .github/workflows/test.yml (shipped under webpack-oq4) so PRs run the build + typecheck before tests. Add 'npm run build' (or 'tsc --noEmit' as a separate typecheck step) ahead of npm test. The release workflow filed under webpack-e5r (paired) already has a npm run build step in the user's template — this bead just guarantees that step has something real to compile. Acceptance: test workflow on a draft PR runs build + typecheck + tests, all green on Node 18/20/22.
acceptance_criteria: test.yml runs build/typecheck before tests; matrix all green on Node 18/20/22

title: chore(ts): add TypeScript tooling — tsconfig, ts-jest, @typescript-eslint
description: Set up the TypeScript toolchain so subsequent src/ migration work has somewhere to land. devDeps: typescript ^5 (latest stable; bump if TS 7 ships first), ts-jest ^29, @types/node, @types/jest, @typescript-eslint/parser ^7, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin ^7. Add tsconfig.json (target ES2020, module CommonJS, outDir ./dist, rootDir ./src, declaration+declarationMap+sourceMap, strict, esModuleInterop). Update .eslintrc.json with @typescript-eslint parser/plugin alongside eslint-plugin-n. Add 'build': 'tsc' script and ts-jest preset. Add dist/ to .gitignore. Add src/types.d.ts with ambient declarations for any deps without published types (e.g. error-stack-parser). Acceptance: npm run build succeeds (dist/ may be empty until child src migration lands), tsc --noEmit clean, eslint passes, all 33 existing tests pass under ts-jest.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build emits to dist/, tsc --noEmit passes, eslint clean, all 33 jest tests pass via ts-jest

title: refactor(ts): migrate src/ from JavaScript to TypeScript
description: Convert all 13 src/ files (friendly-errors-plugin, core/extractWebpackError, core/transformErrors, core/formatErrors, transformers/{babelSyntax,esLintError,moduleNotFound}, formatters/{defaultError,eslintError,moduleNotFound}, output, utils/{index,colors}) plus index.js to TypeScript with proper types. Define core types: AnnotatedError, Transformer = (e: AnnotatedError) => AnnotatedError, Formatter = (errors: AnnotatedError[], type: 'error'|'warning') => string[], PluginOptions. Use webpack's published types for Compiler/Stats/Compilation/MultiStats. Main class implements WebpackPluginInstance. index.ts uses 'export = FriendlyErrorsWebpackPlugin' to preserve dual require()/import compatibility. Tests stay .spec.js (ts-jest resolves TS sources from JS specs); converting tests to .ts is a separate follow-up. Acceptance: tsc --noEmit clean, all 33 tests still pass, _sandbox/demo.js still produces the three demo outputs.
acceptance_criteria: tsc --noEmit clean with no any leaks except where webpack types force them, 33/33 tests pass, _sandbox/demo.js works against built plugin

title: test: restore postcss and mini-css-extract integration fixtures on webpack 5
description:
Three integration tests were skipped in webpack-72l because the loader/autoprefixer upgrades changed the output the captured assertions depended on:

1. integration : mini CSS extract plugin babel error — webpack 5 + mini-css-extract-plugin@2 + sass-loader@13 emits a wrapped pair of errors (sass parse + downstream module-parse from the generated JS) instead of a single clean block. Fixture at test/fixtures/mini-css-extract-babel-syntax/.
2. integration : postcss-loader : warnings — autoprefixer@10 no longer emits 'grid-gap only works if grid-template(-areas) is being used' for the CSS in test/fixtures/postcss-warnings/index.css. postcss-loader@7 also changed its loader path from node_modules/postcss-loader/src/index.js to dist/cjs.js, so the 'Module Warning (from …)' substring is stale even after a new warning is produced. Pick a postcss plugin + input that deterministically emits a warning on the modern toolchain.
3. integration : postcss-loader : warnings (multi-compiler version) — same issue, multi-compiler form.

All three tests are marked it.skip with a comment pointing to this bead. Work is: rebuild fixtures to produce deterministic output, re-enable the tests, update expected strings.

title: docs: update README with Conventional Commits contributing guide and CI badges
description: Update README.md: add a Contributing section with a Conventional Commits cheat-sheet mapping types to release bumps (from the .releaserc releaseRules). Replace dead Travis CI and AppVeyor badges with a GitHub Actions badge. Remove the stale 'TODO: Make it compatible with node 4' line. Part of Slice 3.
close_reason: README updated: swapped Travis/AppVeyor badges for GitHub Actions test-workflow badge, added Contributing section with Conventional Commits cheat-sheet mapping types to release bumps, removed stale node-4 TODO.

title: chore: set package.json version to 0.0.0-semantic-release and add publishConfig.access=public
description: Two small package.json changes that pair with the .releaserc: set version to '0.0.0-semantic-release' (semantic-release overwrites on publish — keeping a real version there invites drift), and add 'publishConfig': { 'access': 'public' } (required for the scoped @soda/... name). Do NOT add semantic-release as an npm script — it only runs in CI. Do NOT add semantic-release or any @semantic-release/* plugin as devDependencies — they run exclusively inside the GitHub Action via extra_plugins. Part of Slice 3.

title: feat: add .releaserc with conventional-commits preset and custom release rules
description: Add .releaserc (extension-less, JSON content — semantic-release auto-detects format) with the plugin config specified in docs/MODERNIZATION.md Slice 3. Plugins: commit-analyzer (conventionalcommits preset + custom releaseRules: chore=no-release, perf/compat/style=patch, refactor=minor, BREAKING CHANGE=major), release-notes-generator with custom section icons, changelog, npm, git (assets: package.json + CHANGELOG.md, commit message chore(release): ...). No @semantic-release/github plugin — GitHub Releases are not created. Part of Slice 3.

title: ci: add GitHub Actions test workflow with Node matrix
description: Create .github/workflows/test.yml. Triggers: pull_request on any branch, push on master. Matrix: Node versions supported by webpack 5 (e.g. [18.x, 20.x, 22.x] — trim to what webpack 5 officially declares). Steps: checkout, setup-node with npm cache, npm ci, npm test. Concurrency group per ref so redundant runs cancel. This workflow is the gate for the follow-up release workflow. Part of Slice 2.

title: chore: modernize _sandbox/ to webpack 5 config syntax
description: _sandbox/webpack.config.js uses webpack 4 'module.loaders' syntax. Rewrite to webpack 5: 'module.rules', explicit 'mode', updated loader configuration. Update _sandbox/package.json deps to webpack 5 + current loaders. Sandbox is excluded from lint and tests per CLAUDE.md — this is a pure config refresh. Part of Slice 2.

title: chore: bump eslintrc ecmaVersion and replace eslint-plugin-node with eslint-plugin-n
description: Update .eslintrc.json: parserOptions.ecmaVersion from 8 (ES2017) to 2022 — enables optional chaining, nullish coalescing, class fields. Replace eslint-plugin-node (unmaintained) with eslint-plugin-n (the maintained fork) in devDependencies and in the extends list. Review plugin:n/recommended rules that reject modern syntax now that the Node floor is higher. Part of Slice 2.

title: chore: upgrade jest to 29.x, eslint to 8.x, chalk to 4.x
description:
Upgrade jest from 24.x to 29.x (current major). Upgrade eslint from 5.x to 8.x (keep .eslintrc.json; flat-config migration is out of scope). Bump chalk from 3 to 4 (chalk 5 is ESM-only, this package ships CommonJS). Part of Slice 2.

NOTE: CSS/SCSS/PostCSS loader bumps (css-loader, sass-loader, postcss-loader, style-loader, autoprefixer, mini-css-extract-plugin) were folded into webpack-72l because the webpack@5 pin required their majors to match.

title: chore: remove dead devDependencies (babel-6, eslint aliases, node-sass, etc.)
description: Trim package.json devDependencies that remain dead on the webpack-5 toolchain. After webpack-72l landed, only the eslint-7 alias is left — and that alias is intentionally kept because test/fixtures/eslint-webpack-plugin-warnings pins its ESLint via require.resolve('eslint-7'). Once webpack-sul bumps the root eslint to 8.x, revisit whether the alias can be collapsed. If still needed, close this without further changes.

title: test: verify full integration suite against webpack 5
description: Run 'npm test' end-to-end with webpack 5 pinned, every fixture refreshed, memory-fs replaced, and internal imports removed. Every captured output assertion in test/integration.spec.js must still match exactly (whitespace included — NODE_ENV=test suppresses timestamps so equality holds). Sign-off gate for Slice 1. Depends on all other Slice 1 issues.

title: compat: bump engines.node to match webpack 5 floor
description: Change package.json engines.node from '>=8.0.0' to whatever the pinned webpack 5 currently declares (at writing: '>=10.13.0'). Rule: never require a newer Node than webpack itself. Part of Slice 1.

title: compat: replace memory-fs with memfs in integration tests
description: memory-fs@0.4.1 is incompatible with webpack 5. Remove the memory-fs devDependency and port test/integration.spec.js to use memfs directly (it exposes the Volume API that webpack 5 accepts). The existing tests only use memory-fs to read compiled output back — the replacement is mechanical. Part of Slice 1.

title: compat: pin devDependency webpack to ^5 and upgrade loader fixtures
description: Bump devDependencies.webpack from ^4.31.0 to ^5.x. Walk every test/fixtures/<name>/webpack.config.js to confirm it boots on webpack 5. Swap eslint-loader (deprecated on webpack 5) for eslint-webpack-plugin in any fixtures that use it. Drop the babel-loader-7 alias and any fixture that exists only to cover Babel 6 errors (fixtures using babel-core 6.26.3). Part of Slice 1.
close_reason: Landed on webpack@5 devDep pin with loader-version bumps folded in (css-loader@6, sass-loader@13, postcss-loader@7, style-loader@3, mini-css-extract-plugin@2, autoprefixer@10). Dropped Babel-6 fixture and eslint-loader fixture; redundant deps removed (babel-core, babel-loader-7, babel-preset-react, node-sass, expect, eslint-loader). Switched yarn→npm with .npmrc legacy-peer-deps=true. moduleNotFound transformer updated to handle webpack-5 ModuleNotFoundError shape. 30/33 integration tests green; 3 skipped and tracked under webpack-c5b.

title: compat: replace webpack/lib/RequestShortener import in extractWebpackError.js
description: src/core/extractWebpackError.js:4 imports webpack/lib/RequestShortener — a webpack internal API with no stability guarantee in webpack 5. Replace with an inline duck-typed { shorten(id) } object (strip process.cwd() prefix, normalize separators, strip leading ./). readableIdentifier(shortener) only requires that interface. Call sites: extractWebpackError.js:40 (e.module.readableIdentifier) and :59 (current.issuer.readableIdentifier). Verify with: grep -r 'webpack/lib' src/ returns no matches. Part of Slice 1.

title: compat: drop webpack 4 peer dep and compiler.plugin() fallback
description: Remove the webpack 4 support branch from src/friendly-errors-plugin.js:66-74 (the 'else { compiler.plugin(...) }' fallback) and narrow peerDependencies.webpack in package.json from '^4.0.0 || ^5.0.0' to '^5.0.0'. Webpack 5 always exposes compiler.hooks so the fallback is dead code. This is the breaking change that justifies the 2.0.0 major bump. Part of Slice 1 of the modernization plan (docs/MODERNIZATION.md).

title: chore: add commitlint + husky commit-msg hook
description: Optional hardening: add @commitlint/cli + @commitlint/config-conventional + husky (v9 simple-hook style) with a commit-msg hook. Keeps PRs from landing with non-conforming messages that would confuse the semantic-release analyzer. Can be deferred as a follow-up after the rest of Slice 3 ships. Part of Slice 3 (optional).

value: User runs 'npm i' themselves (always). Agent can run 'npm test' (and other read-only npm scripts), but must NOT pipe to tail/head — output buffering hides real-time progress. Use redirect-to-file (e.g. '> /tmp/test.log 2>&1') and read the file with the Read tool. Always prepend PATH: export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin:$PATH"

semantic-release runs CI-only: no semantic-release or @semantic-release/* devDependencies in package.json. Plugins are provided via GitHub Action extra_plugins at CI time. Keeps local install lean and makes 'does it release?' a CI-only question.

CI workflows that need repo secrets (NPM_TOKEN, etc.) or involve live publishes should be filed as separate beads and paired with the user. Not executed solo — user wants to be in the loop for the first live release and secret provisioning.

node/npm/npx/yarn are zsh shell FUNCTIONS in the Bash-tool's snapshot — lazy-load wrappers that source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh on first call. That sourcing hangs/is very slow per fresh shell. Bypass by calling the binary directly: PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin:$PATH" $HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin/npm <args>. (PATH still needs the nvm bin dir for npm's child node processes.) The user runs 'npm i' themselves; the agent can run 'npm test' / read-only npm scripts using this pattern. Do NOT just run 'npm' or 'export PATH=...; npm' — that hits the zsh function and hangs.

The project switched from yarn to npm during webpack-72l. A .npmrc at repo root sets legacy-peer-deps=true because eslint-webpack-plugin@2 peer-requires eslint@7||8 while the root still has eslint@5 (root eslint bump is webpack-sul). When webpack-sul lands, revisit whether .npmrc is still needed.
